lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
Valentinian goes into the West; Valens remains at Constantinople, and grants the Request of the Macedonians to hold a Synod, but persecutes the Adherents of the 'Homoousion.'

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Valentinian goes into the West; Valens remains at Constantinople, and grants the Request of the Macedonians to hold a Synod, but persecutes the Adherents of the 'Homoousion.'

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Of the emperors one, i.e. Valentinian, speedily went to the western parts of the empire; for the exigencies of affairs required his presence thither: meanwhile Valens, residing at Constantinople, was addressed by most of the prelates of the Macedonion heresy, requesting that another Synod might be convened for the correction of the creed.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The emperor supposing they agreed in sentiment with Eudoxius and Acacius, gave them permission to do so: they therefore made preparations for assembling in the city of Lampsacus.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But Valens proceeded with the utmost despatch toward Antioch in Syria, fearing lest the Persians should violate the treaty into which they had entered for thirty years in the reign of Jovian, and invade the Roman territories.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: They however remained quiet; and Valens employed this season of external tranquillity to prosecute a war of extermination against all who acknowledged the homoousion.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Paulinus their bishop, because of his eminent piety, he left unmolested.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Melitius he punished with exile: and all the rest, as many as refused to communicate with Euzoïus, he drove out from the churches in Antioch, and subjected to various losses and punishments.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: It is even affirmed that he caused many to be drowned in the river Orontes, which flows by that city.
